title: SB 1120 requires physicians to make the final decisions on what kind of treatments patients should receive rather than artificial intelligence

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1120 mandates physician oversight when algorithms are used to decide on healthcare providers' requests to offer medical services.",
    "Artificial intelligence is an important tool in diagnosing and treating patients.",
    "An algorithm does not fully know and understand a patient’s medical history and needs.",
    "The Physicians Make Decisions Act ensures that the human element will always determine quality medical treatments for patients.",
    "Artificial intelligence can enhance health care delivery.",
    "Health insurance plans have increasingly relied on artificial intelligence (AI) to streamline the processing of claims and prior authorization requests.",
    "There are legitimate concerns about inaccuracies and biases in AI used in healthcare.",
    "Wrongful denial of insurance claims based on AI algorithms can lead to serious health consequences, including death.",
    "Leading healthcare organizations have called for oversight over the use of AI in insurance.",
    "The Physicians Make Decisions Act requires a licensed physician to supervise decisions made by algorithms.",
    "Algorithms cannot replicate the nuanced decision-making process of a physician."
  ]
}
```
